question: With eight B200 GPUs available after the current run, which OPD library and Qwen3.5 teacher model are most suitable for SII-Agent OPD?

why: It has first-class OPD: student rollouts plus teacher token log-probs, added as a reverse-KL style penalty on top of GRPO/PPO/REINFORCE-style training.
It directly connects Megatron training with SGLang rollout/teacher serving and explicitly supports Qwen3.5, Qwen3.5 MoE, Qwen3 MoE, Qwen2.5, DeepSeek V3/R1, and Llama.

Its tau-bench example already demonstrates agentic multi-turn tool-use training, assistant-token loss masks, and environment/tool observation masking, which matches the SII-Agent failure mode we saw with answer-only DPO.
Eight B200 GPUs make the extra integration cost more worthwhile because we can run actor/training, student rollout, and a strong SGLang teacher concurrently.
opd_library_recommendation.why_not_primary: It is easier for a quick custom multi-turn GRPO prototype and has excellent scheduler/loss-mask hooks, but OPD teacher KL is not the first-class path we need; we would have to implement teacher forced-logprob KL ourselves.
opd_library_recommendation.why_not_now: It has the strongest fully async service architecture and OPD/agentic RL support, but it is heavier than slime and better suited once we need multi-node or production-grade async clusters.

Qwen3.5-9B.note: Good student baseline; not the best teacher because larger Qwen3.5 models dominate most agent/search benchmarks.

Qwen3.5-27B.note: Best middle-size model on VITA-Bench, HLE-with-tool, Seal-0, and SWE-bench Verified; cheap dense fallback teacher.

Qwen3.5-35B-A3B.note: Highest middle-size TAU2-Bench and very cheap active compute; good fast teacher for tool-use style.

Qwen3.5-122B-A10B.note: Best practical high-quality teacher: strongest middle-size BFCL, DeepPlanning, BrowseComp, BrowseComp-zh, and Terminal Bench 2.

Qwen3.5-397B-A17B.note: Best overall teacher by agent/search benchmarks, but serving cost is high. Use FP8/quantized SGLang if stable; BF16 likely consumes most or all eight B200s.

teacher_recommendation.reason: It is the best practical balance of quality, memory, and concurrent online OPD throughput on eight B200s. It dominates middle-size Qwen3.5 on BFCL, BrowseComp, BrowseComp-zh, DeepPlanning, and Terminal Bench 2.
teacher_recommendation.reason_fast: Very cheap active compute and strong TAU2/tool-use score, useful for debugging OPD rollout/masking before expensive teacher runs.
teacher_recommendation.reason_best: Strongest official Qwen3.5 agent/search model, especially TAU2, VITA, BrowseComp, WideSearch, MCP-Mark, and SWE-bench. Use once SGLang/vLLM forced logprob serving is validated in FP8/quantized mode.

suggested_8xb200_layouts.allocation: 2 GPUs teacher, 2 GPUs student rollout, 4 GPUs actor/training/reference

suggested_8xb200_layouts.goal: Debug SII multi-turn masking and reward/KL plumbing quickly.

suggested_8xb200_layouts.teacher: Qwen3.5-397B-A17B FP8 TP4 or quantized TP4
suggested_8xb200_layouts.allocation: 4 GPUs teacher, 2 GPUs student rollout, 2 GPUs actor/training
suggested_8xb200_layouts.goal: Highest-quality OPD teacher after pipeline is stable.

sources: THUDM/slime README, OPD example, and tau-bench agent example
redai-infra/Relax README and OPD documentation checked earlier
